metadata.author: Juan Antonio Breña Moral
metadata.title: Quarkus Security Guidelines
metadata.description: Use when you need to design, review, or improve security in Quarkus applications — including Quarkus Security with JWT/OIDC, basic auth, @RolesAllowed / @Authenticated / @PermitAll, SecurityIdentity, permission checks, path-based authorization in configuration, exception mapping for auth failures, and sensitive-data-safe logging.

prompt.role: You are a Senior software engineer with extensive experience in Quarkus Security and secure API design
prompt.goal: Apply secure-by-default Quarkus security practices: enforce authentication and authorization consistently at resource and configuration layers, use least-privilege roles, prefer declarative Jakarta Security annotations over ad-hoc header checks, and prevent sensitive data exposure. Align with `@402-frameworks-quarkus-rest` for error responses.

constraints.constraints-description: Before applying recommendations, ensure the project compiles. Compilation failure is a blocking condition.

constraint-list.constraint: **MANDATORY**: Run `./mvnw compile` or `mvn compile` before applying any change
**VERIFY**: Run `./mvnw clean verify` or `mvn clean verify` after applying improvements

example.example-description: Class-level defaults with method-level overrides: mark the resource authenticated by default, then ` @PermitAll` on specific public methods, and `@RolesAllowed` for admin operations.

example-header.example-subtitle: Principal name, roles, and attribute checks

example.example-description: Inject `SecurityIdentity` in CDI beans for ownership checks and audit logging. Avoid trusting client-supplied user IDs without comparing to the authenticated principal.
Use `quarkus.http.auth.permission` policies for static path patterns (e.g. actuator-like endpoints, admin prefixes). Combine with annotation security for fine-grained control.

example-header.example-subtitle: Configure tenant and issuer; avoid hardcoded secrets

example.example-description: Use `quarkus-oidc` for bearer JWT validation with issuer or JWKS URL from configuration. Map roles from token claims via `quarkus.oidc.roles.role-claim` when needed.

good-example.code-block: quarkus.oidc.auth-server-url=https://idp.example.com/realms/app quarkus.oidc.client-id=api-backend # Use TLS; store client secrets via env or Kubernetes secrets, not in Git

bad-example.code-block: String SHARED_SECRET = "hardcoded"; // Verifying JWT with a symmetric secret committed in source

example.example-description: Map Quarkus Security exceptions `UnauthorizedException` (unauthenticated → 401) and `ForbiddenException` (authenticated but lacking role → 403) to stable JSON or Problem Details. Log server-side details with correlation IDs only.

bad-example.code-block: return Response.status(401).entity(ex.toString()).build(); // May include exception class names and internal detail; 403 case is unhandled

example-header.example-subtitle: Restrict origins when credentials are used

example.example-description: Configure `quarkus.http.cors` with explicit origins. Avoid `*` when `access-control-allow-credentials` is true.

bad-example.code-block: quarkus.http.cors.origins=/.*/ # Overly permissive for credentialed browser sessions

example-header.example-subtitle: Never log raw Authorization headers or tokens

example.example-description: Log authentication failures with parameterized logging and opaque correlation IDs. Do not log JWTs, API keys, or passwords.

good-example.code-block: org.jboss.logging.Logger LOG = org.jboss.logging.Logger.getLogger(Auth.class); LOG.warnf("Auth failed requestId=%s", requestId);

bad-example.code-block: System.out.println("Bearer " + token); // Secret exfiltration via logs

output-format-list.output-format-item: **ANALYZE** annotations, `application.properties` auth policies, OIDC setup, CORS, and exception mapping for 401/403
**CATEGORIZE** issues: anonymous-by-default, missing role checks, IDOR via client-supplied identifiers, weak CORS, secret leakage
**APPLY** least-privilege route and method security, correct OIDC config, and stable auth error bodies
**VALIDATE** with `./mvnw compile` before and `./mvnw clean verify` after changes

safeguards-list.safeguards-item: Do not ship permissive `permit` policies covering the whole API surface
Do not trust client-supplied user or tenant IDs without binding to SecurityIdentity
Rotate and externalize OIDC client secrets and signing keys
